prompt: Camera move left. A vast desert canyon opens in stepped layers of sandstone, its walls cut into buttresses, narrow ledges, and dark vertical fissures that reveal millions of years of erosion. In the near ground, fractured shale and scattered boulders form a sharp foreground plane, while farther mesas recede through bands of dusty blue haze. A dry wash snakes along the canyon floor, with a few persistent pools catching pale morning light. Wind lifts thin veils of sand from exposed shelves, and sparse juniper clings to cracks in the rock, emphasizing the scale, depth, and rigid geological structure across the scene.

Camera move right. An alpine lake stretches along a glacial valley, bordered by smooth granite slabs, pebble beaches, and clusters of low pines rooted in thin soil. The water near shore is glass-clear, revealing submerged stones, wavering strands of aquatic plants, and the slope of the lakebed before it deepens to a cold cobalt center. Small wind ripples distort reflections of snow-streaked peaks, while a meltwater stream enters over polished rocks, throwing white spray and circular ripples across the surface. Patches of lingering ice hug the shaded bank under bright, high summer air.

Camera orbit left. A solitary basalt sea stack rises just offshore from a storm-beaten headland, ringed by black boulders and tide channels etched into the volcanic shelf. Late autumn weather rolls through in layers: a low bank of rain over the horizon, brighter breaks in the cloud deck, and shafts of weak golden light touching the wet stone. White surf bursts against the stack and drains back in silver sheets, while wind combs the marram grass on the cliff above. The sky keeps shifting from slate gray to luminous blue gaps, creating a demanding interplay of shadow, glare, mist, and reflected color.

Camera orbit right. A monumental limestone arch dominates a high plateau, its weathered span bridging two eroded towers above a field of broken talus. The rock surface is rich with pockets, bedding lines, and streaks of iron staining, while beneath the opening the ground drops into a maze of gullies and smaller fins extending toward distant buttes. Thin grasses and hardy shrubs occupy protected crevices, giving scale to the immense stone structure. Warm sunset light grazes one face of the arch, leaving the opposite side in cool shadow, and far-off dust in the basin softens the horizon into layered depth.
